frameworks.name: unequal-variance signal detection theory
frameworks.one_line: Detection model with asymmetric signal vs noise distribution variances

frameworks.name: equal-variance signal detection theory
frameworks.one_line: Canonical d-prime model assuming equal signal and noise variances

frameworks.name: response-time-based SDT
frameworks.one_line: Uses RT distributions to recover SDT parameters in lieu of confidence

core_question: Can response times substitute for confidence ratings in estimating unequal-variance SDT parameters in detection tasks?
key_assumptions: Type-1 ROC asymmetry reflects greater variability in stimulus-present trials
RT distributions carry sufficient information to recover SD ratio and mean difference
Confidence-based estimates serve as a benchmark for SDT parameter recovery
manipulations: RT-based vs confidence-based parameter estimation method
Equal-variance d-prime vs unequal-variance d_a as sensitivity index
key_findings: RT-based estimates of SD ratio and mean difference aligned with confidence-based estimates
d_a derived from RT and from confidence showed strong consistency across datasets
Conventional d-prime consistently overestimated detection performance relative to d_a
ROC asymmetry indicates greater signal variability in stimulus-present trials
interpretations: RT-based SDT is a reliable, cost-effective alternative when confidence ratings are unavailable
Accounting for unequal variance is essential for precise detection-performance evaluation
